goal: Match sim stereo camera intrinsics to real Hawk module (fx=958.57, baseline 150mm, 2064x1544).
thoughts: USD cameras don't expose fx directly — derive via fx = (focalLength / horizontalAperture) * width_px. Pick horizontalAperture that matches real sensor width, then solve for focalLength. Baseline fix: translate the RIGHT camera prim by +baseline in x, don't rotate the stereo parent. USD radial distortion: Isaac Sim cameras are pinhole by default; real distortion needs the fisheye/omnidirectional camera model or post-process in the perception node. Disparity 2px offset is usually principal-point (cx) asymmetry.
code:
# 1. Locate L/R cameras
cams = list_all_prims(type_filter="Camera", path_contains="front_stereo")
left, right = cams[0].path, cams[1].path

# 2. Derive focalLength from target fx
# fx = (focalLength / horizontalAperture) * width_px
# Pick horizontalAperture matching real Hawk sensor width (mm)
H_APERTURE = <SENSOR_WIDTH_MM>
WIDTH_PX   = 2064
TARGET_FX  = 958.57
FOCAL      = TARGET_FX * H_APERTURE / WIDTH_PX

for cam in [left, right]:
    set_camera_params(
        prim_path=cam,
        focal_length=FOCAL,
        horizontal_aperture=H_APERTURE,
        vertical_aperture=H_APERTURE * 1544 / 2064,
        resolution=[2064, 1544],
    )

# 3. Set baseline by translating RIGHT camera only
set_attribute(prim_path=right, attribute="xformOp:translate", value=[0.150, 0, 0])

# 4. Verify via /camera_info
ros2_list_topics()
run_usd_script(code="import rclpy; # echo /front_stereo_camera/left/camera_info, confirm K[0]~958.57")
failure_modes: assistant tries to set fx directly as a USD attribute
assistant rotates the stereo parent to set baseline
assistant promises full radial distortion on pinhole camera
assistant confuses principal-point offset with baseline
